title: linaria alpina var. petraea
intro: Linaria alpina var. petraea, commonly known as alpine toadflax, is a flowering plant species in the family Plantaginaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, where its medicinal properties are attributed to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of alpine toadflax include its potential to alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to support wound healing and reduce fever. Alpine toadflax can be prepared as a tea or tincture, and it is also used in herbal infusions, allowing for various applications in traditional medicine and holistic health practices.
